paintings.inspiration: Painted during the first weeks of spring, Ananda — Sanskrit for 'bliss' — radiates outward from a golden core through rings of burnt sienna and soft coral, finishing in deep midnight blue.
paintings.subtitle: Original Mandala Painting
paintings.title: Ananda

paintings.inspiration: Shanti means peace. Cool teals and soft lavenders layer outward in perfect symmetry, evoking the stillness of a mountain lake at dawn.
paintings.subtitle: Original Mandala Painting
paintings.title: Shanti

paintings.inspiration: Named for the sun, Surya blazes in deep ochres and saffrons, a painting that commands a wall and fills a room with warmth.
paintings.subtitle: Original Mandala Painting
paintings.title: Surya

paintings.inspiration: Chandra — the moon — rendered entirely in silvers and ivory whites on a deep charcoal ground. Sold, but I'd love to create something in a similar spirit.
paintings.subtitle: Original Mandala Painting
paintings.title: Chandra

paintings.inspiration: Dharani means 'she who holds'. Soft pinks and sage greens bloom outward like petals — created during a period of deep gratitude.
paintings.subtitle: Original Mandala Painting
paintings.title: Dharani

paintings.inspiration: Akasha — ether, infinite space — is the largest in the Sacred Geometry collection. Deep indigos and ultramarines suggest a cosmos folded into perfect symmetry.
paintings.subtitle: Original Mandala Painting
paintings.title: Akasha

paintings.inspiration: A small but energetic piece — vibrant magentas and turmeric yellows radiate life force from a tight, intricate centre.
paintings.subtitle: Original Mandala Painting
paintings.title: Prana

paintings.inspiration: Metta means loving kindness. Earthy terracottas and warm greens create a grounded, tender composition — perfect for a bedroom or meditation space.
paintings.subtitle: Original Mandala Painting
paintings.title: Metta

paintings.inspiration: Four sacred creatures — tortoise, peacock, owl, and elephant — each held within their own mandala medallion, stacked in a tall vertical scroll. A celebration of the animal world through the language of Madhubani folk art.
paintings.subtitle: Original Madhubani Painting
paintings.title: Chatushpada

paintings.inspiration: Aditya — the sun — painted in the bold lines of folk tradition. Fiery red and orange rays burst outward from a serene face set against an intricate black henna-work ground. Painted on paper, this piece carries the raw energy of an older practice.
paintings.subtitle: Original Folk Painting
paintings.title: Aditya

paintings.inspiration: Vivah — the sacred union. A long horizontal Madhubani panel depicting a wedding procession: women bearing gifts, musicians playing, and the bride seated beneath a ceremonial canopy, surrounded by flowering vines. One of the largest and most detailed works in the collection.
paintings.subtitle: Original Madhubani Painting
paintings.title: Vivah

paintings.inspiration: Two peacocks face each other in perfect symmetry, tail feathers fanned, beaks almost touching — a symbol of love and fidelity. Set against a golden Madhubani border of leaves and blossoms.
paintings.medium: Ink and acrylic on canvas board
paintings.subtitle: Original Madhubani Painting
paintings.title: Mayura Yugma

paintings.inspiration: Two women sit together beneath a blossoming tree in quiet conversation — a peacock perches above, a fish swims in the border. Painted in the Madhubani tradition, this piece is an ode to friendship, nature, and the stories shared in stillness.
paintings.subtitle: Original Madhubani Painting
paintings.title: Sakhiyaan

paintings.inspiration: A larger, more refined canvas rendering of the sun deity — Bhaskar, the luminous one. The same bold folk tradition reimagined on stretched canvas, the intricate black mehndi-work ground giving way to bold saffron and coral rays.
paintings.subtitle: Original Folk Painting
paintings.title: Bhaskar

paintings.inspiration: Kamdhenu — the sacred wish-fulfilling cow of Hindu tradition — painted in the Pichwai folk style. A white cow adorned with garlands, a mandala blanket, and golden anklets walks beside a pink lotus on a deep terracotta ground. A painting of grace, abundance, and devotion.
paintings.subtitle: Original Folk Painting
paintings.title: Kamdhenu

paintings.inspiration: A digital mandala created in deep navy and steel blue — concentric petals of precise geometry radiating outward from a still sapphire centre. Printed on archival paper, signed Art Vasu.
paintings.subtitle: Limited Edition Print
paintings.title: Neela
